qa_pairs.question: When is it appropriate to use whole-node scheduling for serial work?
qa_pairs.answer: You are welcome to use whole-node scheduling if you have huge amounts of serial work and can efficiently use META-Farm, GNU Parallel, GLOST, or other techniques to pack serial processes onto a single node.

qa_pairs.question: What is the maximum run-time for jobs on Trillium?
qa_pairs.answer: Trillium accepts jobs with a maximum run-time of up to 24 hours.

qa_pairs.question: What is the maximum run-time for jobs on Fir, Narval, Nibi, and Rorqual?
qa_pairs.answer: Fir, Narval, Nibi, and Rorqual accept jobs with a maximum run-time of up to 7 days.

qa_pairs.question: Which clusters allow jobs to run for up to 7 days?
qa_pairs.answer: Fir, Narval, Nibi, and Rorqual allow jobs to run for up to 7 days.

qa_pairs.question: Are there different job time limits for various clusters?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, Trillium accepts jobs of up to 24 hours run-time, while Fir, Narval, Nibi, and Rorqual accept jobs of up to 7 days.
